2009-12-01.title: Clinging to the Forest Despite the Chaos
2009-12-01.abstract: Tribes in remote Venezuela face the challenges of a thriving bush-meat trade, incursions by gold miners and government control over their land.

2009-12-01.title: Europe’s Foreign Policy Guide Passes His Compass
2009-12-01.abstract: Javier Solana, who built the institutions of a European Union foreign service as its policy chief, will yield Tuesday to a less experienced figure.

2009-12-01.title: United Nations Requests $7.13 Billion for 2010 Humanitarian Work
2009-12-01.abstract: Afghanistan rose to second place after Sudan among the countries most in need. Drought in West Africa was also of particular concern.

2009-12-01.title: A Plan to Save a Forest From an Unlikely Source
2009-12-01.abstract: A paper company proposes to use a ring of industrial tree plantations to help stop leaks of greenhouse gases from a peat bog.

2009-12-01.title: Oil Companies Look to the Future in Iraq
2009-12-01.abstract: Major oil companies are finally gaining access to Iraq’s petroleum reserves, but at far less advantageous terms than they once envisioned.

2009-12-02.title: Obama Adds Troops, but Maps Exit Plan
2009-12-02.abstract: President Obama said he would begin to draw U.S. forces out of Afghanistan in July 2011, even after sending some 30,000 more troops by mid-2010.

2009-12-02.title: Climatologist Leaves Post in Inquiry Over E-Mail Leaks
2009-12-02.abstract: The head of the British research unit at the center of a controversy over the disclosure of e-mail messages among scientists has stepped down.

2009-12-02.title: Obama’s Address on the War in Afghanistan
2009-12-02.abstract: The transcript of President Obama’s address at the United States Military Academy on a new strategy for the war in Afghanistan.

2009-12-02.title: Russia: Investigator Wounded During Bomb Inquiry
2009-12-02.abstract: A bomb exploded at the site of an attack on a luxury train, one day after another bomb derailed the train between Moscow and St. Petersburg, killing 26 people.

2009-12-02.title: France: Algerian Freed From Guantánamo Prison
2009-12-02.abstract: Saber Lahmar, 39, had been held for seven years at the American prison at Guantánamo Bay, arrived in France on Tuesday.

2009-12-03.title: Taking the Pulse of Military Blogs After the President’s Speech
2009-12-03.abstract: A round-up of reaction from military blogs to President Obama’s new plan in Afghanistan.

2009-12-03.title: H.I.V. Tests Turn Blood Into Cash in China
2009-12-03.abstract: An ambitious H.I.V. prevention program financed by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation in China is unusual because it offers a financial incentive.

2009-12-03.title: Indonesia: Forest Mismanagement Costs Billions
2009-12-03.abstract: A study of government and forest industry figures between 2003 and 2006 said that illegal logging, tax evasion by exporters and unreported subsidies resulted in a total loss of $6 billion.

2009-12-03.title: Mexico: Protected Witness Killed in Starbucks
2009-12-03.abstract: Gunmen in Mexico City on Tuesday killed a former federal police official who had been arrested for corruption last year and had become a protected witness, officials said.

2009-12-03.title: Russia: Chechen Separatists Claim Responsibility for Train Bombing
2009-12-03.abstract: The separatists said they were seeking to strike at economically important targets in Russia.

2009-12-04.title: At Least 3 Die in Syria Blast; Officials Call It an Accident
2009-12-04.abstract: Government officials and witnesses gave conflicting reports about the cause of the explosion and the death toll.

2009-12-04.title: Court Papers Detail Killings by the Military in Guatemala
2009-12-04.abstract: Military reports filed in a case brought in Spain describe the deaths of Mayan civilians during a campaign against leftist guerrillas in the 1980s.

2009-12-04.title: C.I.A. to Expand Use of Drones in Pakistan
2009-12-04.abstract: The White House is expanding the program, paralleling the decision to send more troops to Afghanistan.

2009-12-04.title: India: Lagging in Fighting Measles
2009-12-04.abstract: India is the global laggard in reducing measles deaths, with three-quarters of the 164,000 deaths from measles that still occur worldwide annually, the United Nations said.

2009-12-04.title: Vatican: Greater Ties With Russia
2009-12-04.abstract: The Holy See and the Russian Federation vowed to establish full diplomatic ties, the Vatican said Thursday, after a meeting between Pope Benedict XVI and Russia’s president, Dmitri A. Medvedev. The Vatican called the meeting “cordial.” Ties between the Vatican and Russia have historically been strained, because of the cold war and the centuries-old rift between the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches. In spite of improved ties, Vatican officials said an official visit to Russia was not likely, The Associated Press reported.

2009-12-05.title: Fire Sweeps Through Club in Russia, Killing 100
2009-12-05.abstract: A fire, apparently caused by an indoor pyrotechnical display, roared through a crowded nightclub in the Russian city of Perm, the local media reported.

2009-12-05.title: Cholera Epidemic Infects Thousands in Kenya
2009-12-05.abstract: The most stricken areas are the arid swaths of northern Kenya, which were hit this year by a devastating drought, and where many are surviving off dirty water.

2009-12-05.title: Another Clue to How China Managed Obama’s Visit
2009-12-05.abstract: An organizer of a “town hall” session said there had been concerns about embarrassing guests and upstaging meetings with President Hu Jintao.

2009-12-05.title: G.I.’s Learning to Stand Down as Iraqis Step In
2009-12-05.abstract: American soldiers’ role in urban areas has changed, meaning they now must let local forces handle security, even if imperfectly.

2009-12-05.title: Cultural Differences
2009-12-05.abstract: “The officers don’t want to do it, because they say we have a lot of experience and we don’t need it.”

2009-12-06.title: Arrests in Philippine Province
2009-12-06.abstract: Dozens were arrested after martial law was imposed on the province where a recent massacre left 57 people dead.

2009-12-06.title: Copenhagen Talks Tough on Climate Protest Plans
2009-12-06.abstract: An elaborate holding facility with three dozen steel cages has been created to accommodate more than 350 potential troublemakers during a U.N. climate conference Monday.

2009-12-06.title: Morales Headed for Re-election in Bolivia
2009-12-06.abstract: President Evo Morales seems to be sailing to re-election as his indigenous movement continues to shake institutions.

2009-12-06.title: Gates Calls July 2011 the Beginning, Not End, of Afghan Withdrawal
2009-12-06.abstract: Perhaps only a “handful” of U.S. troops will leave Afghanistan in 2011, Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said.

2009-12-06.title: Victory Claimed by Both Candidates in Romania
2009-12-06.abstract: Mircea Geoana, the former foreign minister, declared victory. The incumbent, President Traian Basescu, refused to concede.

2009-12-07.title: Venezuela Takes Greater Control of Banks
2009-12-07.abstract: The government detained a powerful financier and forced the resignation of his brother who is a top aide to President Hugo Chávez.

2009-12-07.title: No Firm Plans for a U.S. Exit in Afghanistan
2009-12-07.abstract: Top administration officials said Sunday that any troop pullout beginning in July 2011 would be slow.

2009-12-07.title: Mothers Arrested Before Opposition Rally in Iran
2009-12-07.abstract: Iran arrested more than 20 mothers who were mourning children killed in the unrest since the disputed elections.

2009-12-07.title: After Delays, Deal Set on Iraq Election Law
2009-12-07.abstract: Lawmakers in Iraq brokered a last-minute compromise that will allow for the first national elections since 2005.

2009-12-07.title: Voices from the Time Zone Debate
2009-12-07.abstract: A sampling of comments from the Russian-language blog of The New York Times, as well as other blogs on livejournal.com, as translated by the Moscow bureau of The Times.

2009-12-08.title: In Search of Former Glory, a City Welcomes Its Midas
2009-12-08.abstract: An industrial city prepares for a visit from Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin, who has been seen as a savior of factory towns for swooping in to boost local economies.

2009-12-08.title: Pressure From All Sides as Karzai Picks His Team
2009-12-08.abstract: The Afghan leader faces a choice between rewarding the people who helped him secure his disputed re-election, or answering demands to curb corruption.

2009-12-08.title: Years Later, Destruction of a Mosque Still Echoes
2009-12-08.abstract: The demolishing of the mosque at Ayodhya, India, in 1992 by a Hindu mob set off riots in which 2,000 people died, most of them Muslims.

2009-12-08.title: Greenhouse Gases Imperil Health, E.P.A. Announces
2009-12-08.abstract: The agency said the gases posed a danger to human health and the environment, paving the way for regulation of emissions.

2009-12-08.title: 6 Accused in 1982 Poisoning Death of Chilean Leader
2009-12-08.abstract: Three people tied to the Pinochet dictatorship were charged with murder in the death of a former Chilean president, Eduardo Frei Montalva, and three others were charged with attempting to cover up the crime.

2009-12-09.title: Obama Administration Takes a New Approach to Biological Weapons
2009-12-09.abstract: The Obama administration plans to announce a new policy to curb the spread of biological weapons, but it will reaffirm the Bush administration’s opposition to international verification of stockpiles of various agents.

2009-12-09.title: Turkey Is Considering Ban on Kurdish Party
2009-12-09.abstract: Parliament’s only pro-Kurdish party, accused of undermining national stability and cooperating with militants, could be shut down if its members are convicted in a court case under way.

2009-12-09.title: Japan Suspends Talks on Where to Move a U.S. Air Base
2009-12-09.abstract: The suspension of talks over relocating a United States air base on Okinawa is a sign of renewed disagreement over an issue in an increasingly rocky relationship between Tokyo and Washington.

2009-12-09.title: Spilling the Blood of Bulls to Preserve Zulu Tradition
2009-12-09.abstract: The bare-handed killing of a bull by Zulu warriors is part of an annual ceremony in South Africa, but this year an animal rights group took up the cause of the doomed bull.

2009-12-09.title: Canada: A Taser Death Is Re-examined
2009-12-09.abstract: A civilian oversight agency strongly criticized the actions and credibility of Royal Canadian Mounted Police officers who were involved in a fatal encounter with a bewildered and disoriented Polish immigrant.

2009-12-10.title: American Support for Afghan War Plan Grows
2009-12-10.abstract: A bare majority of Americans support President Obama’s plan to send 30,000 additional troops to Afghanistan, but many are skeptical about Afghanistan’s partnership in the fight.

2009-12-10.title: China’s Economic Power Unsettles the Neighbors
2009-12-10.abstract: China is finding it harder to cast itself as a friendly alternative to an imperious American superpower.

2009-12-10.title: And in This Corner, Climate Contrarians
2009-12-10.abstract: At their own forum in Copenhagen, climate change skeptics displayed as much passion for their cause as activists pushing for urgent action on global warming.

2009-12-10.title: Pakistan Detains Five Americans in Raid Tied to Militants
2009-12-10.abstract: Five young Muslim American men who had disappeared were detained in Pakistan in a raid linked to a militant group.

2009-12-10.title: Petraeus Warns of a Long and Expensive Mission in Afghanistan
2009-12-10.abstract: America’s role in Afghanistan could last for years and cost upward of $10 billion annually just to finance an adequate Afghan security force, a top general said.

2009-12-11.title: Kurdish Rebels Claim Ambush as Their Deed
2009-12-11.abstract: A radical wing of the separatist Kurdish rebel group claimed responsibility for an attack last week that killed seven Turkish soldiers and raised tensions in the country.

2009-12-11.title: Mexico: Rebuke on Investigation of Murders
2009-12-11.abstract: The Inter-American Court of Justice accused the Mexican government of inaction in preventing, investigating and prosecuting the murders of young women in the border state of Chihuahua.

2009-12-11.title: Blackwater Guards Tied to Secret C.I.A. Raids
2009-12-11.abstract: The guards’ involvement blurred the lines dividing the C.I.A., the military and the private security company.

2009-12-11.title: Quake Threat Leads Swiss to Close Geothermal Project
2009-12-11.abstract: A study deals a blow to the idea of energy from hot rock deep in the earth, as a similar project in California faces review.

2009-12-11.title: Stolen From the Dead
2009-12-11.abstract: Grave robbers are looting Venezuela's cemeteries for human bones, answering demand from practitioners of a Cuban religion called Palo that uses the bones in its ceremonies.

2009-12-12.title: Turkey Bans Kurdish Party
2009-12-12.abstract: ISTANBUL — Turkey’s constitutional court disbanded the only pro-Kurdish party in Parliament on Friday, a move that could threaten efforts to resolve the conflict with the Kurdish minority by peaceful means.

2009-12-12.title: Violence Grips South Sudan as Vote Nears
2009-12-12.abstract: With a referendum on independence planned, over 2,000 people have been killed in southern Sudan this year.

2009-12-12.title: Rock ’n’ Roll Explains a Star’s Life Under Communism
2009-12-12.abstract: Jan Josef Liefers, a German TV star, is using the rock music of his youth to voice his feelings about growing up in East Germany.

2009-12-12.title: F.B.I. Questions 5 Americans Detained in Pakistan
2009-12-12.abstract: It was not known whether the men, suspected of pursuing jihadist training, would be deported to the United States.

2009-12-12.title: New Incidents Test Immunity to Terrorism on U.S. Soil
2009-12-12.abstract: The rampage in Fort Hood, Tex., and the account of Virginia men who went to Pakistan challenge the idea that U.S. Muslims are less vulnerable to radicalization than those in Europe.

2009-12-13.title: Protesters Mostly Quiet After Day of Action
2009-12-13.abstract: Scattered protests continued on Sunday, but climate activists in Copenhagen were largely quiet after a day of mass demonstrations resulted in nearly 1,000 arrests.

2009-12-13.title: Officials Seek Destination of North Korean Arms
2009-12-13.abstract: The crew of a cargo aircraft traveling from North Korea said they did not know they were carrying an arsenal weighing at least 30 tons.

2009-12-13.title: Jarno Trulli and Heikki Kovalainen at Lotus
2009-12-13.abstract: Jarno Trulli and Heikki Kovalainen at Lotus?

2009-12-13.title: Ambitious Rivers Project Meets a Sea of Opposition
2009-12-13.abstract: A project to remake South Korea’s four longest rivers has spurred a national debate over what constitutes green development.

2009-12-13.title: An Attack on Silvio Berlusconi
2009-12-13.abstract: Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi was struck in the face after a political rally in Milan on Sunday and rushed from the scene with blood on his face.

2009-12-14.title: Defiling of Pictures of Khomeini Divides Iran
2009-12-14.abstract: Iran’s hard-line leadership admonished protesters for damaging pictures of Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, but the opposition said accusations were fabricated.

2009-12-14.title: President of Abkhazia Re-elected by Wide Margin
2009-12-14.abstract: President Sergei V. Bagapsh said his victory reflects a desire to strengthen ties to Russia, and said he does not assign much importance to recognition from the West.

2009-12-14.title: Attacker Fractures Berlusconi’s Nose
2009-12-14.abstract: A man assaulted the Italian prime minister, raising questions about the leader’s security amid a rising tide of criticism.

2009-12-14.title: Airstrikes Kill at Least 35 in Northwestern Yemen
2009-12-14.abstract: A witness said bombs struck a market, apparently killing civilians. Houthi rebels said the strikes were carried out by Saudi warplanes.

2009-12-14.title: Iraq War Inquiry Depicts Britain as Sidekick to U.S.
2009-12-14.abstract: The first three weeks of hearings have done little to blunt a common view in Britain that the war was a historic blunder.

2009-12-15.title: Greek Leader Offers Plan to Tackle Debt Crisis
2009-12-15.abstract: Prime Minister George Papandreou announced ambitious measures to reassure Europe and investors that he had an exit route out of the country’s worst crisis in decades.

2009-12-15.title: Moving the Dead to Make Room for the Living
2009-12-15.abstract: Singapore is campaigning to close cemeteries as it seeks ways to accommodate an expanding population.

2009-12-15.title: Relations With Turkey Kindle Hopes in Syria
2009-12-15.abstract: For Syrians, the new relationship suggests an embrace of a more open society and holds the promise of a revitalized regional economy.

2009-12-15.title: Thailand: Bail Is Denied for 5 on Plane Carrying Arms
2009-12-15.abstract: Five crew members of an aircraft carrying weapons from North Korea were denied bail in Bangkok and ordered held for at least 12 days while the police investigate the destination of the arms shipment.

2009-12-15.title: Corrections
2009-12-15.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article on Wednesday about Israelis’ reactions to a deal being negotiated for the release of Staff Sgt. Gilad Shalit that is expected to free hundreds of Palestinian prisoners referred incorrectly to a war in 1982, after which Israel handed over hundreds of prisoners in exchange for three captured Israelis. It was the Lebanon War  not a war against Hezbollah, which did not exist when Israel invaded Lebanon in 1982.

2009-12-16.title: Delay Is Expected on Treaty Talks
2009-12-16.abstract: The United States does not expect to conclude negotiations with Russia on a nuclear arms treaty in time for an accord to be signed later this week when President Obama is in Copenhagen.

2009-12-16.title: A Market for Beef Ambles Across a Border
2009-12-16.abstract: Political and economic upheaval in Argentina has helped open the door for its neighbor Uruguay to claim the grass-fed mantle in worldwide beef sales.

2009-12-16.title: Abkhazia Is Recognized — by Nauru
2009-12-16.abstract: Facing depletion of its main resource, phosphates, Nauru is only the fourth nation to recognize the region, which separated from Georgia.

2009-12-16.title: Canada: Crime Solved, but It’s Still a Mystery
2009-12-16.abstract: Why a Toronto man stole 2,865 bicycles last year remained a mystery on Tuesday after he pleaded guilty to reduced charges

2009-12-16.title: Turkey: Two People Killed in Kurdish Protests
2009-12-16.abstract: Two people were killed and at least six wounded Tuesday in Kurdish protests that have raged since the only Kurdish party in Parliament was banned by the Constitutional Court last week.

2009-12-17.title: A Legacy of Regret for a Saudi Diplomat
2009-12-17.abstract: Prince Saud al-Faisal, Saudi Arabia’s foreign minister, said his legacy would be one of disappointment because he had failed to help establish a Palestinian state.

2009-12-17.title: Holiday of White Conquest Persists in South Africa
2009-12-17.abstract: As Afrikaners celebrate an 1838 covenant said to have led to the slaughter of 3,000 Zulus, the ideal of a rainbow nation now seems little more than a deft turn of phrase.

2009-12-17.title: U.N. Officials Say American Offered Plan to Replace Karzai
2009-12-17.abstract: As fraud surfaced in the Afghan election, the No. 2 U.N. official in the country proposed enlisting the White House in a plan to replace Hamid Karzai.

2009-12-17.title: Outside Climate Talks, Protesters March on the Hall
2009-12-17.abstract: Demonstrators called for radical measures to curb global warming and sought to enter the conference center to hold a “people’s assembly.”

2009-12-17.title: Corrections
2009-12-17.abstract: Because of an editing error, a Reporter’s Notebook article on Tuesday about international climate talks in Copenhagen erroneously attributed a distinction to the Horns Rev 2 wind farm off the Danish coast, which Mayor Michael R. Bloomberg visited this week on a side trip from the talks. The Horns Rev 2 is the world’s largest offshore wind farm, not the largest wind farm. (The largest is in Roscoe, Tex.).

2009-12-18.title: A Holiday Reflects a Racial Divide
2009-12-18.abstract: The 16th of December is supposed to be the Day of Reconciliation in South Africa. But on this day, descendants of white settlers continue to celebrate an older holiday, commemorating a covenant said to be made between their ancestors and God in 1838 that led to the slaughter of 3,000 Zulus.

2009-12-18.title: China Is Disputing Status of Uighurs in Cambodia
2009-12-18.abstract: After deadly ethnic riots in July in western China and a government crackdown, the 22 Uighurs entered Cambodia and applied for refugee status at a United Nations refugee office.

2009-12-18.title: Brazil Court Halts Boy’s Return to U.S.
2009-12-18.abstract: A regional federal court in Brazil had previously ruled that the 9-year-old should be returned to his father in the United States.

2009-12-18.title: Study Suggests Orphanages Are Not So Bad
2009-12-18.abstract: A new study challenges the belief that orphans in poor countries fare best in family homes in the community and should be put into orphanages only as a last resort.

2009-12-18.title: Corrections
2009-12-18.abstract: An article on Dec. 4 about a detailed analysis of the varnish on Stradivarius instruments may have left the incorrect impression that earlier work by Joseph Nagyvary, a former professor at Texas A&M University, involved just cellos by other makers. When Dr. Nagyvary said, “We never had the privilege of getting a sample from a Strad,” he was referring to violins. (He analyzed a sample of varnish from a Stradivarius cello in 1986.).

2009-12-19.title: One Pakistani Institution Places His Faith in Another
2009-12-19.abstract: Syed Babar Ali, 83, a veteran businessman who helped create the Lahore University of Management and Science, wants to restore merit to Pakistani society.

2009-12-19.title: Afghan President Tries to Please Both Warlords and West
2009-12-19.abstract: As Hamid Karzai presents his list of cabinet nominees to Parliament, he is under pressure to weed out corruption, but he is also beholden to warlords who mustered votes for him, sometimes illegally.

2009-12-19.title: Doubts Grow in Japan About Premier Amid Money Scandal
2009-12-19.abstract: Prime Minister Yukio Hatoyama was reportedly preparing to tell prosecutors that he had no direct role in a campaign finance scandal.

2009-12-19.title: Iran: Opposition Web Site Disrupted
2009-12-19.abstract: Computer hackers disrupted an opposition Web site and Twitter late Thursday and Friday, identifying themselves as the Iranian Cyber Army and posting anti-American messages in English.

2009-12-19.title: Poland: Sign at Auschwitz Is Stolen
2009-12-19.abstract: The iron sign over the gate to the Auschwitz memorial with the phrase “Arbeit macht frei” — “Work sets you free” — has been stolen, the Polish police said Friday.

2009-12-20.title: 6 Detainees Are Returned to Yemen
2009-12-20.abstract: Six detainees who had been held for years at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, were repatriated, in a trial run of a policy that will reduce the number of those held at the facility.

2009-12-20.title: Rebuilding a Monastery in Iraq
2009-12-20.abstract: The United States Army hopes to restore St. Elijah's Monastery, an ancient site of Christian worship stuck in the middle of a military base in northern Iraq.

2009-12-20.title: Abu Ghraib, Restive Again
2009-12-20.abstract: Many Sunni Arabs have yet to reconcile themselves to the loss of power and privileges they enjoyed under Saddam Hussein’s regime

2009-12-20.title: Death of Iranian Cleric Could Set Off New Protests
2009-12-20.abstract: The senior cleric’s funeral on Monday is widely expected to be a catalyst for possibly violent confrontations between protesters and the police.

2009-12-20.title: Iran-Iraq Standoff Over Oil Field Ends
2009-12-20.abstract: Iraq said that Iranian soldiers who had been occupying part of a disputed Iraqi oil field had withdrawn, ending a three-day standoff.

2009-12-21.title: China’s Export of Labor Faces Scorn
2009-12-21.abstract: China is increasingly known for shipping out cheap labor, and a backlash against Chinese workers has grown in Africa and Asia.

2009-12-21.title: Iraqi Qaeda Group Shifts to Remain a Threat
2009-12-21.abstract: Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia is revamping its tactics and strategy to conduct less frequent but increasingly catastrophic attacks.

2009-12-21.title: Amid Scrutiny, Karzai Defends Cabinet Picks
2009-12-21.abstract: President Hamid Karzai of Afghanistan sought to reassure the world that his proposed ministers are professionals rather than political favorites.

2009-12-21.title: Lebanon Drama Adds Act With Leader’s Trip to Syria
2009-12-21.abstract: Prime Minister Saad Hariri’s trip to Syria had historical echoes for many Lebanese, and epitomized their national story.

2009-12-21.title: Nepalese Police Arrest 70 in Clash With Maoists
2009-12-21.abstract: The violence was a new sign that a stalled peace process was unraveling altogether, seven months after the Maoists abandoned the government.

2009-12-22.title: After Expelling Uighurs, Cambodia Approves Chinese Investments
2009-12-22.abstract: Over international objections, China had sought the forced repatriation of 20 Uighurs who fled the country in a government crackdown.

2009-12-22.title: Challenging Traditions at the Heart of Judaism
2009-12-22.abstract: A group of women who meet for prayers at the Western Wall is challenging the Orthodox religious establishment, which operates the holy site.

2009-12-22.title: Russians on New Domains
2009-12-22.abstract: Here are comments by participants on the Russian-language blog of The New York Times on livejournal.com, translated by the Moscow bureau of The Times.

2009-12-22.title: Russians Wary of Cyrillic Web Domains
2009-12-22.abstract: Computer users are worried that a move by the Russian government would end up restricting Internet access.

2009-12-22.title: Russia: A Celebration of Stalin
2009-12-22.abstract: The Communist Party celebrated the 130th birthday of Joseph Stalin on Monday with an appeal for people not to bring up the more unseemly aspects of his record.

2009-12-23.title: Karzai Calls for Inquiry Into Killing of a Relative
2009-12-23.abstract: Some members of the Karzai family have said they believed that Waheed Karzai was killed by a relative, a politically well-connected cousin of President Karzai, as part of a family feud.

2009-12-23.title: Kyrgyz President Blamed in Homicide
2009-12-23.abstract: Gennadi Pavlyuk, a politically active former editor, was bound and thrown from a window while on a visit to a neighboring Central Asian country.

2009-12-23.title: Jordan’s King Remakes His Government
2009-12-23.abstract: King Abdullah II has dismissed the prime minister and replaced him with a palace aide and loyalist, dissolved Parliament and postponed legislative elections.

2009-12-23.title: U.S. Put Jails in Lithuania, Premier Says
2009-12-23.abstract: A report contends that state security officials never informed senior government officials about two small secret prisons.

2009-12-23.title: Tight Times in Ukraine Means Cramped Quarters for Its Zoo Animals
2009-12-23.abstract: The Kiev Zoo aspired to a lofty status but has fallen on hard times for lack of money and attention.

2009-12-24.title: Jordanians Can Take a Joke, Comics Find
2009-12-24.abstract: A show with North American comedians demonstrated that when it comes to humor, there is no clash of civilizations.

2009-12-24.title: The Tire Change – Scenes From 2009
2009-12-24.abstract: The highway became a makeshift pit stop as the Americans hastened to change the wheel before night fell.

2009-12-24.title: Mexico: Arrests in Deaths of Sailor’s Kin
2009-12-24.abstract: Mexican authorities have arrested four people in connection with the fatal attack on Ensign Melquisedet Angulo Córdova’s family on Tuesday.

2009-12-24.title: Congo: Short Extension for U.N. Force
2009-12-24.abstract: The United Nations Security Council took the unusual step on Wednesday of extending the mandate of the 20,000-member peacekeeping force in Congo only until May, instead of for a year.

2009-12-24.title: Eritrea: U.N. Sanctions Imposed
2009-12-24.abstract: The United Nations Security Council embargoed arms exports and imports to and from the east African nation, accusing its government of supporting Islamist militants in Somalia.

2009-12-25.title: Women’s Deaths, and Inquiry’s Findings, Enrage Kashmir
2009-12-25.abstract: The politically explosive case is emblematic of the mistrust between Kashmiris, many of whom want independence, and the Indian government.

2009-12-25.title: Police Try to Quell Protesters Who Mourn Iranian Cleric
2009-12-25.abstract: In Tehran and the northwestern city of Zanjan, officers clashed with demonstrators defying a ban on memorial ceremonies for Grand Ayatollah Hossein Ali Montazeri.

2009-12-25.title: Tough Military Stance Stirs Little Debate in Israel
2009-12-25.abstract: A year after the Gaza offensive, the devastating use of force is seen as an effective deterrent against Hamas and Hezbollah.

2009-12-25.title: Turkey: Kurdish Officials Arrested
2009-12-25.abstract: Two weeks after the disbanding of the pro-Kurdish party in Parliament, Turkish security forces detained dozens of former party members, saying they were linked to a separatist rebel group.

2009-12-25.title: Greece: Austerity Budget Passed
2009-12-25.abstract: Parliament passed a 2010 budget that is aimed at reining in Greece’s soaring deficit by cutting public spending by 10 percent and cracking down on tax evasion.

2009-12-26.title: Iraq: 9 Killed in Scattered Violence
2009-12-26.abstract: Much of the recent violence has been aimed at Shiites making their way to the holy city of Karbala for Ashura, which commemorates the martyrdom of Imam Hussein.

2009-12-26.title: Pakistan: Muslim Americans Stay in Jail
2009-12-26.abstract: A judge in Sargodha ordered that the five men suspected of pursuing jihadist training in Pakistan remain in custody for another 10 days so the police could continue their investigation.

2009-12-26.title: In Iraq, on Holiday, a Round of Attacks on Shiites
2009-12-26.abstract: Insurgents have killed dozens of pilgrims during a Shiite religious observance in an attempt to reignite sectarian violence.

2009-12-26.title: Indonesian Project Shows Obstacles After Tsunami
2009-12-26.abstract: A road in Indonesia is a testament to the extraordinary reconstruction effort — and immense obstacles — in hard-hit Aceh Province in Indonesia since disaster struck.

2009-12-26.title: Tehran Protesters Defy Ban and Clash With Police
2009-12-26.abstract: Demonstrations in Iran underline the government’s inability to suppress the opposition despite beatings with batons and chains.

2009-12-27.title: Police Are Said to Have Killed 10 in Iran Protests
2009-12-27.abstract: The police opened fire on protesters, killing at least 10 people, including a nephew of the opposition leader.

2009-12-27.title: 4 Killed in Iraqi Bombing, but Holiday Is Mostly Calm
2009-12-27.abstract: A bomb killed four people in northern Iraq, but a massive deployment of security forces kept the culmination of the Shiite observance of Ashura largely peaceful.

2009-12-27.title: Violence in Iran
2009-12-27.abstract: Four people were killed by Iranian police as thousands gathered for opposition protests in Iran.

2009-12-27.title: Thailand Begins Repatriation of Hmong to Laos
2009-12-27.abstract: Armed with riot shields and batons, the Thai military began early Monday to forcibly return 4,000 asylum seekers to Laos.

2009-12-27.title: Pakistan Is Rocked by a Spate of Attacks
2009-12-27.abstract: A handful of deadly attacks ravaged parts of Pakistan over the weekend and highlighted the multiple security challenges confronting the embattled Islamabad government.

2009-12-28.title: U.S. Widens Terror War to Yemen, a Qaeda Bastion
2009-12-28.abstract: American officials said Yemen could become Al Qaeda’s next operational and training hub, rivaling the tribal areas of Pakistan.

2009-12-28.title: Women at Arms
2009-12-28.abstract: While serving in Iraq, Capt. Margaret H. White's biggest threat came from a fellow soldier.

2009-12-28.title: A Peril in War Zones: Sexual Abuse by Fellow G.I.’s
2009-12-28.abstract: The integration of women in the military has forced to the surface issues that commanders rarely confronted before.

2009-12-28.title: French Mosque’s Symbolism Varies With Beholder
2009-12-28.abstract: The planned Grand Mosque of Marseille represents Muslim assimilation in France, but is also a symbol of new anxiety among non-Muslim Europeans.

2009-12-28.title: Vietnam Sentences a Dissident to 5 Years in Prison as Part of a Crackdown
2009-12-28.abstract: A court on Monday convicted Tran Anh Kim of subversion for pro-democracy activities and sentenced him to five and a half years in prison.

2009-12-29.title: Georgian Airline Resumes Russia Trips
2009-12-29.abstract: Russia’s Ministry of Transport has given permission for a Georgian airline to resume charter flights between Moscow and Tbilisi before the New Year.

2009-12-29.title: Israel: Ex-Foreign Minister Declines to Join Right-Leaning Government
2009-12-29.abstract: Tzipi Livni, leader of the opposition Kadima Party, on Monday turned down, as expected, an offer from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to join his government.

2009-12-29.title: North Korea Detains American
2009-12-29.abstract: It was not possible to determine whether the person arrested by North Korea was Robert Park, an American missionary who entered the country on Friday.

2009-12-29.title: A Briton Is Executed in China, Britain Says
2009-12-29.abstract: China has executed a British man convicted of drug smuggling after rejecting appeals from relatives who said he was mentally unstable.

2009-12-29.title: Death Toll Climbs in Attack on Pakistani Shiites
2009-12-29.abstract: The death toll from a suicide bomber’s attack on a Shiite religious procession in Karachi, Pakistan, was reported to have risen to 40 on Tuesday.

2009-12-30.title: In Heart of Iraq, a Plan to Revive the Pulse of a Central Artery
2009-12-30.abstract: A $5 billion plan to rebuild Baghdad’s economic and cultural main street is the most ambitious vision put forward of a new Iraq.

2009-12-30.title: Terror Inquiry Looks at Suspect’s Time in Britain
2009-12-30.abstract: A look at Umar Farouk Abdulmutallab’s years in London points out a difficulty in preventing terrorist attacks: no one can identify a trigger moment.

2009-12-30.title: Protesters Gather in Cairo for March to Gaza
2009-12-30.abstract: The march is intended to mark a year since Israel’s military assault on the Palestinian territory, and to protest the blockade of Gaza.

2009-12-30.title: Russia: No Jail for Tax Fraud Suspects
2009-12-30.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev amended the criminal code to make the tax evasion charges used to hold a lawyer who died in pretrial detention no longer punishable by jail time.

2009-12-30.title: U.S. Official Given Access to American Held in Cuba
2009-12-30.abstract: A subcontractor who was arrested at Havana’s airport on Dec. 4 was allowed to meet with a State Department consular official.

2009-12-31.title: Hotel Diplomacy – Scenes From 2009
2009-12-31.abstract: They complained that the United States wanted to hear from them, but seemed to have no sense of their reality.

2009-12-31.title: In Cuba, Hopeful Tenor Toward Obama Is Ebbing
2009-12-31.abstract: President Obama’s election was celebrated, but Cuba’s leaders now portray him as just another imperialist.

2009-12-31.title: Chinese Businesses Resist Eviction by Developers
2009-12-31.abstract: As a real-estate boom has swept the nation, property owners and occupants often protest unfair compensation.

2009-12-31.title: A Mideast Bond, Stitched of Pain and Healing
2009-12-31.abstract: The friendship between two children in a hospital — a Palestinian and an Israeli — has drawn their parents into a kinship that defies national struggle.

2009-12-31.title: In Tehran, Thousands Rally to Back Government
2009-12-31.abstract: The chants of the crowds on Wednesday — and Sunday — spoke to the depth of the schism in Iranian society since the disputed presidential elections in June.
